paper_metadata.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
paper_metadata.abstract: State-of-the-art computer vision systems are trained to predict a fixed set of predetermined object categories. This restricted form of supervision limits their generality and usability since additional labeled data is needed to specify any other visual concept. Learning directly from raw text about images is a promising alternative which leverages a much broader source of supervision. We demonstrate that the simple pre-training task of predicting which caption goes with which image is an efficient and scalable way to learn SOTA image representations from scratch on a dataset of 400 million (image, text) pairs collected from the internet. After pre-training, natural language is used to reference learned visual concepts (or describe new ones) enabling zero-shot transfer of the model to downstream tasks. We study the performance of this approach by benchmarking on over 30 different existing computer vision datasets, spanning tasks such as OCR, action recognition in videos, geo-localization, and many types of fine-grained object classification. The model transfers non-trivially to most tasks and is often competitive with a fully supervised baseline without the need for any dataset specific training. For instance, we match the accuracy of the original ResNet-50 on ImageNet zero-shot without needing to use any of the 1.28 million training examples it was trained on. We release our code and pre-trained model weights at https://github.com/OpenAI/CLIP.

extraction_data.innovation: CLIP learns visual representations by jointly training an image encoder and text encoder to maximize the cosine similarity between matching image-text pairs in a shared embedding space, using contrastive loss. This enables zero-shot transfer to downstream tasks without task-specific fine-tuning.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: 400 million (image, text) pairs collected from the internet
extraction_data.extension: Uses a contrastive objective to align image and text embeddings in a shared multi-modal space, enabling zero-shot transfer to diverse tasks.
extraction_data.research_problem: Learning transferable visual models from natural language supervision
extraction_data.application: Zero-shot image classification, OCR, geo-localization, action recognition
extraction_data.paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

extraction_data.innovation: CLIP ResNet-50x16 is a scaled version of CLIP using ResNet-50 with 16x compute, further improving performance through model scaling.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, zero-shot transfer, multimodal understanding
extraction_data.research_problem: Transferable visual models from natural language supervision
extraction_data.extension: Uses ResNet-50 with EfficientNet-style scaling, increasing width, depth, and resolution to improve performance.
extraction_data.paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

extraction_data.innovation: CLIP ResNet-50x64 is a scaled version of CLIP using ResNet-50 with 64x compute, demonstrating the benefits of large-scale model scaling.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, zero-shot transfer, multimodal understanding
extraction_data.research_problem: Transferable visual models from natural language supervision
extraction_data.extension: Uses ResNet-50 with EfficientNet-style scaling, increasing width, depth, and resolution to improve performance.
extraction_data.paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

extraction_data.innovation: CLIP ViT-B/32 is a Vision Transformer-based CLIP model with 32x32 patch size, demonstrating the effectiveness of ViT in multimodal learning.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, zero-shot transfer, multimodal understanding
extraction_data.research_problem: Transferable visual models from natural language supervision
extraction_data.extension: Uses Vision Transformer architecture with 32x32 patch size and layer normalization on combined patch and position embeddings.
extraction_data.paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision

extraction_data.innovation: CLIP ViT-L/14 is a large Vision Transformer-based CLIP model with 14x14 patch size, demonstrating the effectiveness of ViT in multimodal learning.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, zero-shot transfer, multimodal understanding
extraction_data.research_problem: Transferable visual models from natural language supervision
extraction_data.extension: Uses Vision Transformer architecture with 14x14 patch size and layer normalization on combined patch and position embeddings.
extraction_data.paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
